flights.outbound: SAS SK4744/4438 · BRU 17:05 → Oslo → Tromsø, land 22:50 (Sat 1 Aug)
flights.return: SAS SK4453/0597 · TOS 15:00 → Copenhagen → BRU, home 19:50 (Fri 14 Aug)

timedAnchors.what: Fly home · TOS → CPH → BRU, land 19:50

blocks.text: Land in Tromsø on SK 4744 / 4438 and check in — first taste of the never-quite-dark Arctic night.
A full day to acclimatise: ride the Fjellheisen cable car (or climb the Sherpa steps) for the view over the island city, cross to the Arctic Cathedral, and wander the harbour and Polaria.
Shops shut on Sunday, so just grab essentials — the big provisioning run waits for Monday.
Collect the camper and roll into Day 1.

days.title: Tromsø → Senja

blocks.text: Pick up the camper at Arctic Campers in Kvaløysletta — a quick walk-through of the van, then load up.
Big grocery shop in Tromsø while the supermarkets are finally open after Sunday — stock the van for the days ahead.
Drive west toward Senja, pulling in at Tungeneset's wind-carved rocks and the postcard beach at Ersfjord.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Settle at the Fjordgård / Mefjord campsite, cast off the pier and fire up the first BBQ of the trip.

days.noteHTML: Booked: Camp Mefjord, Camp 1 spot 06 (ref 2008) — check-in from 14:00. Confirmation is in Gmail.

days.title: Segla day, stage for the ferry

blocks.text: — an early climb for the trip's signature view, Segla's blade rising straight out of the sea (~4.5–5h).
Back at the van for a long lunch, legs earned.
Take the slow scenic road toward Gryllefjord, stopping at the Bergsbotn viewpoint over the fjord.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: A loose afternoon by the water near the quay — sleep close to the ferry for an easy morning.

days.title: First ferry → Bleik

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Join the Gryllefjord ferry queue with breakfast brewing in the van and a line in the water while you wait.
The ferry sails the open-sea crossing to Andenes (whales are a real chance) — arrive 12:40.
Settle in early at Midnattsol Camping, Bleik, before the afternoon spots fill.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Bleik beach and an optional Måtind walk; mackerel off the pier — or take the boat out to Bleiksøya's puffin colony.

days.noteHTML: Fallback: miss the 11:00 and the next sailing is 15:00 — but expect Bleik to be busier by then.

blocks.text: A slow morning on Andøya — the Bleik beach, the Måtind ridge, or coffee in Andenes before you roll south.
Drive south through Vesterålen's gentler, rolling scenery.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Find a quiet, legal spot near Sortland for a free night by the fjord — a slower day to let the trip breathe.
Sortland to Melbu, then the short Melbu–Fiskebøl ferry (~25 min, roughly hourly) onto the archipelago.
Drive into Lofoten proper and on to Svolvær, the islands’ capital.
above town — the classic look down over Svolvær and the 'Devil's Gate' wedged between two slabs of rock (~3h).

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Settle at Sandsletta Camping; cast from the harbour walls.
early while the small trailhead car park is quiet — a steep, short pull to one of Lofoten's best viewpoints (~2.5–3h).
Drop into Henningsvær — galleries, good coffee, the football pitch out on the rocks, the working harbour. (Lot full? Do the village first, hike later.)

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Drift out toward a scenic Gimsøy pitch; fish the Sundklakk bridge current.

days.title: Haukland & Uttakleiv

blocks.text: (or Mannen) for the panorama over the turquoise bays (~2–2.5h).
Claim a spot at the Haukland beach car park (first-come) and let the whole afternoon be yours.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Walk the coastal path over the headland to Uttakleiv — two of Norway's finest beaches, back to back — and fish the shoreline.

days.noteHTML: Camping in nature: vans park in the beach lot (first-come, sometimes restricted) — Uttakleiv's simple self-service pitch over the headland is the backup. Grill on the sand, not the grass.

days.title: Kvalvika & Ryten → Beach Camp

blocks.text: At the Fredvang trailhead before the parking crunch.
for the iconic clifftop view down onto Kvalvika's wild beach, dropping to the sand before turning back (~4–5h).
Roll into Lofoten Beach Camp on Skagsanden and claim your pitch now — it’s first-come and fills fast, so be in early, not at the 18:00 cut-off. Book your surf session at check-in.

blocks.label: Surf
blocks.text: Surf Skagsanden — board and wetsuit straight from the camp, right on the sand.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Sauna (to 22:00) and a sea dip, then dinner and a beer at the beach bar (to 23:00); cast off the beach in the late light.

days.noteHTML: The spot Lisa flagged: surf · sauna · beach bar on the sand — and a perfect base for tomorrow's Reinebringen.

days.title: Reinebringen & Reine

blocks.text: before the stair traffic — the Sherpa staircase to the most photographed view in Lofoten (~2.5–3h).
Down by late morning; base at Moskenes Camping.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Drift through the villages at the end of the road — Reine, Hamnøy, Sakrisøy and Å — with deep water for fishing right beside the tarmac. (Optional guided kayak under the granite walls.)

days.noteHTML: Suggested stops: Å at the very end of the E10 — turf-roofed rorbuer, ~5 km on — is an easy stroll to fold into today. For more, the Reinefjord ferry (park at Reine, no cars aboard) crosses to Vindstad for the easy ~1 h hike to Bunes beach: a full day-trip on its own, or camp wild on the dunes overnight. Book the ferry ahead in summer.

blocks.text: Depart east on the E10 — the long road home begins, so cue up the albums.
Break it with a Svolvær viewpoint and a fjord stop or two.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Find a scenic, free pitch near Narvik or Bjerkvik for a last quiet night by the water — one final cast.
An easy drive north with the jagged Lyngen Alps across the fjord; a few coffee-and-photo stops.
Pull into Tromsø Lodge & Camping.
Empty tanks, refuel and repack so the last morning is clean.

days.title: Van back 10:00 · fly 15:00

blocks.text: Empty the tanks, repack, and one last camp coffee.
Return the van at Arctic Campers, Kvaløysletta.
Transfer to town/airport (depot → TOS ~25 min); a last harbour stroll if there’s time.
Fly TOS → Copenhagen → Brussels (SK 4453 / 0597), home 19:50.

days.noteHTML: Comfortable: the 10:00 drop leaves ~5 h before the flight.

stops.label: Tromsø · start / end
stops.note: Land late; two easy nights in town — Fjellheisen and the harbour.

stops.label: Senja · N1
stops.note: Pick up the van, big shop, drive to Senja for a pier-side BBQ.

stops.label: Gryllefjord · N2
stops.note: Climb Hesten for the Segla view, then stage by the ferry quay.

stops.label: Andenes / Bleik · N3
stops.note: Morning ferry to Andøya; beach and mackerel fishing at Bleik.

stops.label: Sortland · N4
stops.note: A slow morning on Andøya, then a scenic Vesterålen night.

stops.label: Svolvær · N5
stops.note: Ferry into Lofoten and hike Fløya & Djevelporten above town.

stops.label: Gimsøy · N6
stops.note: Early Festvågtinden, then galleries and coffee in Henningsvær.

stops.label: Haukland · N7
stops.note: A short summit, then the Haukland–Uttakleiv beach walk.

stops.label: Beach Camp · N8
stops.note: Hike Ryten over Kvalvika, then surf and sauna at the beach camp.

stops.label: Reine · N9
stops.note: Dawn Reinebringen stairs, then potter around Reine and Å.

stops.label: Å i Lofoten · N9 side-trip
stops.note: Museum fishing village at the very end of the E10 — turf-roofed red rorbuer, ~5 km past Reine. Easy same-day stroll.

stops.label: Reinefjord ferry · from Reine
stops.note: MS Fjordkyss from Reine harbour to Kjerkfjord & Vindstad (~25 min). No cars aboard — park at Reine; book ahead in summer via Reis Nordland.

stops.label: Bunes Beach · via Vindstad
stops.note: White-sand beach under Helvetestinden — ferry to Vindstad, then an easy ~1 h hike over a low pass. Wild camp on the dunes behind the beach.

stops.label: Narvik · N10
stops.note: The big drive off the islands east to a fjord spot near Narvik.

campsites.name: Camp Mefjord
campsites.note: Booked ✓ ref 2008 · Camp 1 spot 06 · kr 400 · check-in from 14:00.

campsites.name: Midnattsol Camping
campsites.note: No pitch reservations (cabins only) — self check-in machine. Settle in early on ferry day.

campsites.name: Sandsletta Camping
campsites.note: Booked ✓ & paid · Campcation #17839508906 · pitch w/ electricity · kr 450.

campsites.name: Moskenes Camping
campsites.note: Van pitches book online (Campio); tent pitches can't pre-book in summer. Fills mid-afternoon.

campsites.name: Tromsø Lodge & Camping
campsites.note: Strongly recommend online booking; fills in summer.

ferries.booking: No pre-booking — queue on the quay; campervans queue overnight
ferries.note: Catch the ~11:00 (arrive Andenes ~12:40). Be in the 08:30 queue.

ferries.season: Island route up to ~4/day in summer
ferries.note: Free for your vehicle if you disembark at Værøy. Værøy is a full day (Håheia hike). Exact per-date times: Torghatten live booking picker.

items.label: Camper · all-in, paid (#TOS12792)
items.sub: incl. Golden day, CV insurance, kit, service & toll fee

items.label: Flights · Brussels ⇄ Tromsø × 2 (est.)
items.sub: SAS via Oslo / Copenhagen · Standard, bags incl.

items.label: Campsites × 7
items.sub: incl. Lofoten Beach Camp ~530 w/ power

items.label: Beer · supermarket, 2+ pp/day
items.sub: ~40 kr/can vs 90–130 in a bar · wine from home

budget.note: "Per day" averages across 11 days; "per person" splits every line in two. The Tromsø stay is the real figure (€227); flights are still an estimate.

activities.title: Puffin safari, Bleiksøya
activities.textHTML: 90 min among ~80,000 puffin pairs with sea eagles overhead — it leaves from the harbour right by your campsite. Do it the afternoon you arrive. puffinsafari.no

activities.title: Arctic surf
activities.textHTML: The world's northernmost surf scene, right on Skagsanden where you're sleeping — boards, wetsuits, lessons and a warm-up sauna on site, no driving. Book the session at check-in. lofotenbeachcamp.no

activities.title: Kayak Reinefjorden
activities.textHTML: A ~3 h guided paddle under the granite walls, with a free sauna after — a perfect Reine afternoon. reinepaddling.no

fromHome: Your nice wine — same tax-free cap whether bought here or at the airport, so bring the bottles you want: up to 4 each (8 total) if you skip spirits. Pad them in the checked bags.
Good coffee & BBQ spices/marinades — dear up north.
Charcuterie + a hard cheese for the first nights (up to 10 kg meat/cheese pp from the EEA).
You each have 23 kg checked — room for a soft cooler bag.
inNorway: Bring vs buy: groceries run ~25% above home (meat & dairy worst, fish barely) — so bring cured meat, hard cheese, coffee & BBQ spices; buy fresh & bulky (produce, bread, beer) locally and catch the fish.
Big shop in Tromsø on Monday (Day 1) — Sunday shops shut. Then Sortland, Leknes, Svolvær.
Beer top-up at supermarkets (~40 kr/can vs 90–130 in a bar); sales stop ~20:00 weekdays, ~18:00 Sat, none Sun.
Fresh produce, bread, dairy — and the fish you catch.

checklist.heading: Fishing
items: Travel rod — Caperlan WXM-500 Travel 2.40 m (4-section, packs for flying)
Mackerel feathers + a few 40–60 g pilks

goodToKnow: No northern lights yet — too bright in early Aug.
Midnight sun just ended; days are ~endless.
The van's tall — watch height-barrier car parks.
Treat hike days as swappable around the weather.
Mobile data: Norway's in EU "roam like at home" — your plan works at home rates.
prep.dutyFreeHTML:
The play · duty-free quota

Bring your own wine from home (up to 4 bottles each if you skip spirits), top up beer at Oslo's arrivals tax-free. At ~2 glasses each a night, 8 bottles lasts ~10 nights, then switch to beer. Buy everything fresh and bulky locally; run the BBQ on free, fresh-caught fish.

Standard mix1 L spirits + 1.5 L wine (2 btl) + 2 L beer · per person
Skip spirits→ 3 L wine (4 btl) + 2 L beer
Beer only→ 5 L beer (15 × 0.33)

Trade "down" only (spirits → wine/beer), never up. You can bring more but must declare it and pay Norwegian duty (steep). Plus NOK 6,000 of other goods each.

fuel: Reliable diesel in the towns: Tromsø, Finnsnes, Andenes, Sortland, Svolvær, Leknes.
Thin stretches: Senja's interior, the far SW (Reine → Å), and the long return.
Many northern pumps are unmanned (card only) and close evenings/Sundays — keep the tank above half.
~21 kr/L; ~10–11 L/100 km → ~3,000 kr for the loop. Check AdBlue.
rainDay: Senja: Tungeneset & Bergsbotn viewpoints from the van; Ånderdalen boardwalks.
Lofoten: Lofotr Viking Museum, the Aquarium at Kabelvåg, Vikten glassblowing, a sauna day.
Anytime: swap a summit for the Haukland–Uttakleiv coast path.
driving.breakdown: Save the 24/7 Arctic Campers number day 1; recovery via Viking / NAF.
essentials: Breakdown: save the 24/7 Arctic Campers number day 1; recovery via Viking / NAF.
Road & weather: vegvesen.no (or 175); yr.no.
Hospitals: UNN Tromsø, Harstad, Stokmarknes, Gravdal/Leknes. Bring your EU card.
Mountains: tell someone your plan; turn back in bad weather. On a trail, dial 112.
driving.lead: Keeping fuel in the tank, a plan for the wet days, and the numbers you hope you never need.

overview.splitNote: Camper, flights & the Tromsø stay are locked in; the rest flexes with what you do (set it on the Budget page).

rail.title: Tromsø · land & settle
rail.sub: Land late; two easy nights in town — Fjellheisen and the harbour.

rail.title: Tromsø → Senja
rail.sub: Pick up the van, big shop, drive to Senja for a pier-side BBQ.

rail.title: Hesten → Gryllefjord
rail.sub: Climb Hesten for the Segla view, then stage by the ferry quay.

rail.title: Ferry → Andenes / Bleik
rail.sub: Morning ferry to Andøya; beach and mackerel fishing at Bleik.

rail.title: Andøya → Sortland
rail.sub: A slow morning on Andøya, then a scenic Vesterålen night.

rail.title: Into Lofoten · Svolvær
rail.sub: Ferry into Lofoten and hike Fløya & Djevelporten above town.

rail.title: Festvågtind · Henningsvær
rail.sub: Early Festvågtinden, then galleries and coffee in Henningsvær.

rail.title: Haukland & Uttakleiv
rail.sub: A short summit, then the Haukland–Uttakleiv beach walk.

rail.sub: Hike Ryten over Kvalvika, then surf and sauna at the beach camp.

rail.sub: The big drive off the islands east to a fjord spot near Narvik.

rail.title: Back to Tromsø
rail.sub: Easy drive north past the Lyngen Alps to a Tromsø campsite.

rail.title: Van back · fly home
rail.sub: Return the van at 10:00 and fly home in the afternoon.

drive.note: D3 & D5 add a ferry on top; D1, D10 & D11 are the long hauls.

hike.note: All rated Hard except Offersøykammen (Moderate). Swap around the weather.

daylightHTML:
~19 h
daylight · early Aug
↑ ~03:50sun barely dips~22:45 ↓

Midnight sun's just ended, but at 69° N the sun only grazes the horizon — long twilight bridges the gap, narrowing to ~17 h by the 14th. Hike late, shoot golden hour near midnight, leave the head-torch in the van.
plans: Sun shops shut — essentials only; big shop after pickup
Hotel done — nothing else to reserve
Camp Mefjord, Mefjordvær · kr 400 · booked ✓
Big grocery shop in Tromsø; diesel at Finnsnes
Barbecue hut + outdoor kitchen on site ✓
Camp Mefjord booked ✓ (ref 2008, spot 06) — check-in from 14:00
Last shop at Finnsnes/Senja; fill diesel
Bring-your-own — grill on the quay/rock, clear of trees
Park near the quay for tomorrow’s ferry queue
Diesel + groceries in Andenes on arrival
Barbecue grills + shared kitchen on site ✓
Midnattsol takes no pitch bookings — first-come, arrive early · Ferry pay-on-board ~kr 1,985 (6–8 m) · be in the 08:30 queue for 11:00 · puffin ~kr 500 pp
Bring-your-own — gas or grill on gravel/shore
Nothing fixed — a free morning on Andøya before the drive south
Sandsletta Camping · kr 450 · booked ✓
Sandsletta booked ✓ & paid (Campcation #17839508906) · Melbu–Fiskebøl ferry pay on board, ~hourly

Cinnamon buns at Kringla or Duus Matverk (Svolvær), coffee at Brent. Right by your Sandsletta stop in Laukvik: Kafé Naust, with the Keans Beans coffee distillery next door, plus Livland Gård for homemade ice cream. Boat day → a silent electric Trollfjord cruise or an eagle/orca RIB (Brim Explorer · XX Lofoten), or the ferry over to Skrova island.— from @helenemoo’s Lofoten guide
Top up in Svolvær; small shops in Henningsvær
Nothing to book — free scenic pitch on Gimsøy

Trevarefabrikken in Henningsvær — hip café with weekend brunch and pizza, and a sauna with a big ocean-view window; grab a bun at Lysstøperiet. On the water, a RIB out with Lofoten Opplevelser hunting sea eagles and orcas. On Gimsøy, Hov Gård’s barrel sauna sits right on the beach — and climbers can take on Svolværgeita with a Henningsvær guide.— from @helenemoo’s Lofoten guide
Big shop in Leknes before the beach
Grill on the sand at Haukland, clear of the grass
First-come; overnighting in the lot can be restricted — Uttakleiv’s basic self-service pitch is the backup

Lofoten bakeri in Leknes on the shop run. Worth the detour to Unstad: the guide crowns Arctic Surf’s cinnamon bun the best in Lofoten (huge — share one), right by the famous surf beach; on the way, Lofoten Gårdsysteri farm shop for its own cheese, and the easy coastal walk or viewpoint at Eggum.— from @helenemoo’s Lofoten guide
Lofoten Beach Camp, Skagsanden · ~kr 480 + 50 power
Stock up in Leknes before Flakstad
Beach bar on site; grill on Skagsanden sand
No camper booking — claim pitch by ~14:00 (fills fast); book surf at check-in

Glasshytta at Vikten — watch glass being blown and grab a cinnamon bun; Ramberg’s wide beach and its roadside viewpoint are close by. Detour south to Nusfjord, one of Norway’s best-preserved fishing villages, with Hansines bakeri and the Landhandleriet café.— from @helenemoo’s Lofoten guide
Fuel/shop in Leknes earlier (limited out west); small shop in Reine
Covered BBQ huts + guest kitchen on site ✓
Book Moskenes van pitch online via Campio (fills mid-afternoon) · optional kayak ~kr 2,200 · Reinebringen 07:30 for parking

Å bakeri claims Lofoten’s best cinnamon buns at the literal end of the road; Anita’s Seafood at Sakrisøy for its famous fishcakes and fish burger; Bringen for coffee in Reine. On the water: SUP and snorkelling at Sakrisøy (Schibevaag) or a fishing/RIB trip from Reine (Aqua Lofoten).— from @helenemoo’s Lofoten guide
Fill up before the long E10; diesel in Narvik
Tromsø Lodge and Camping · ~kr 400–450
Barbecue facilities + common kitchen on site ✓
Book Tromsø Lodge online in advance — fills in summer · empty tanks tonight, refuel before drop
Top up diesel near the depot before the 10:00 return
activityExtrasHTML:
Free & unforgettable

  • A "midnight" summit — Reinebringen or Festvågtind at 21:00–23:00 for golden light and an empty trail.
  • Sauna + sea dip — the Nordic recovery ritual; saunas at Sandsletta (D5), Lofoten Beach Camp (D8) and Reine (D9).
  • Fish straight off the boat — buy the day's cod or halibut dockside at Henningsvær or Ballstad.
  • One full-album drive — the Day-10 run off the islands is made for no-skip listening.

Free fish for the BBQ

Saltwater fishing is free and licence-free — shore, pier, rocks or boat. Your hand-line is all you need. August's easy targets:

  • Mackerel (makrell) — inshore Jul–Sep, off any pier; best on the grill.
  • Coalfish (sei) — everywhere off rocks & harbour walls.
  • Pollock (lyr) — kelp edges near dusk; white, flaky.
  • Cod (torsk) — jig deeper water near bridge currents.

The rules & best stops

  • Handheld tackle only; stay 100 m+ from fish farms.
  • Mind minimum sizes; eat it there (no selling, no big export).
  • Rivers/lakes need a fiskekort — stick to the sea.
Best shore stops

Husøy & Gryllefjord (Senja) · Andenes breakwater & Bleik · Svolvær/Kabelvåg harbours · Sundklakk bridge · Henningsvær & Ballstad piers · Fredvang bridges · Uttakleiv/Eggum · Reine·Hamnøy·Å roadside deep water.
